steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Command of Evidence question asking for a quotation that supports the underlined claim. The underlined claim says Saramago's novel "resembles classic magical realist novels in its juxtaposition of literary realism with folklore." The key word is juxtaposition — the quotation must show how realism and folklore are placed side by side and interact within the novel.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '"While Baltasar and Blimunda alternates between realistic and antirealistic modes..., details suggesting the influence of Portuguese folklore nevertheless occur throughout the novel."': This acknowledges both elements are present but frames folklore as "nevertheless" occurring — as if despite the alternating modes. It doesn't show the two elements being placed against each other to create an effect; it describes their coexistence, not their productive tension. ✗
  • Choice B — '"Although Portuguese folklore clearly informs the style..., the novel also shows the inarguable influence of the magical realist tradition of Latin America."': This focuses on identifying literary influences on Saramago rather than demonstrating how folklore and realism interact within the novel itself. Naming the tradition that influenced the work does not show the juxtaposition at work in the text. ✗
  • Choice C — '"The logic of the realistic plot of Baltasar and Blimunda is repeatedly and productively disrupted by the presence of imagery and situations drawn from Portuguese folklore."': This directly shows the juxtaposition: the realistic plot is placed against — and disrupted by — folkloric elements. "Repeatedly and productively disrupted" captures exactly how the two modes interact, matching the claim's emphasis on juxtaposition. ✓
  • Choice D — '"Like many works in the Latin American magical realist tradition, Baltasar and Blimunda is indebted to antirealistic elements in the folkloric tradition of Portugal."': This establishes that the novel belongs to the magical realist tradition and draws on folklore, but "indebted to" describes influence, not juxtaposition. It doesn't show how realism and folklore are placed against each other within the novel. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
